description: A founder reasoning about product strategy, competitive positioning, and decisions over time. Tests causal recall of strategic decisions, competitive facts, cross-product positioning, and abstention on unrecorded facts.

memories.text: The portfolio strategy is a chain: letX, then Quantum Sketch, then Context-Heavy, then common-knowledge. Each product feeds the next, and they interconnect rather than standing alone.
Context-Heavy is cloud-first and private. common-knowledge is local-first and open source. The two share data through the ck bridge but ship on opposite deployment models.
Decision: keep Context-Heavy undeployed until its retrieval engine beats competitor benchmarks. Shipping weak numbers would hurt positioning more than shipping late.
The competitive bar from published numbers: Mem0 scores 94.4 on LongMemEval and 92.5 on LoCoMo; Supermemory 92.3 on LongMemEval-S; Zep 63.8 on a stricter harness.
The target audience for Context-Heavy is anyone — normal users, developers, founders, and working professionals — and any AI agent they use. It is not built for one person.
GBrain is the primary benchmark competitor to beat. It claims a +31 precision-at-5 lift coming from its knowledge graph.
Efficiency is a first-class product metric: every benchmark score is weighted by token cost and latency, not accuracy alone. The thesis is the most efficient brain, not just the most accurate.

questions.question: Why is Context-Heavy not deployed yet?
questions.answer: Because the plan is to wait until its retrieval engine beats competitor benchmarks rather than ship weak numbers.

questions.question: Which competitor is the main benchmark target, and what does it claim?
questions.answer: GBrain, which claims a +31 precision-at-5 lift from its knowledge graph.

questions.question: How do Context-Heavy and common-knowledge differ in their deployment models?
questions.answer: Context-Heavy is cloud-first and private; common-knowledge is local-first and open source.

questions.question: How much funding has the company raised so far?
Who is the target audience for Context-Heavy?
questions.answer: Anyone — normal users, developers, founders, professionals — and any AI agent they use.

questions.question: What is the core product thesis around efficiency?
questions.answer: To be the most efficient brain, not just the most accurate — scores are weighted by token cost and latency.
